title: let-in binding
manager: `let x = ... in ...` for naming a subexpression locally without reaching for mutable state, so a complex guard or assignment can introduce a readable intermediate name that exists only for that expression. Pure, scoped, and side-effect-free, which is what keeps the expression analyzable by the checker instead of becoming a little imperative program hiding inside a transition.
cs: `let`-bindings scoped to the `in` body; immutable; by-value; no mutation escapes the expression.
